title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 13
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: You have been asked to write a report for a local business association about the impact of a new out-of-town shopping mall on small local businesses. Evaluate the current situation and suggest how local shops can compete.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report: The Impact of the 'Grand Plaza' Mall on Local Retail

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to analyse the economic consequences of the recently opened 'Grand Plaza' shopping centre on the independent retailers of our town and to propose strategies for their survival.

Current Economic Climate
Since the mall’s opening six months ago, local high street footfall has decreased by approximately 25%. Small businesses, particularly those in the clothing and electronics sectors, have reported a significant downturn in revenue. The primary draw of the mall appears to be its free parking and the convenience of having multiple global brands in a single, climate-controlled environment. Consequently, many local shopkeepers are struggling to cover their overheads.

Competitive Advantages of Local Shops
Despite the mall’s dominance, local shops possess unique strengths that the 'Grand Plaza' lacks. These include personalised customer service, deep product knowledge, and a sense of community identity. Surveyed residents indicated that they still value the 'human touch' of independent stores but are often swayed by the lower prices offered by larger chains.

Recommendations for Competitiveness
To remain viable, I suggest local businesses adopt the following strategies:
- Digital Presence: Establishing a collective 'Shop Local' online platform where customers can browse inventory and choose home delivery.
- Loyalty Schemes: Implementing a shared loyalty card among independent retailers to encourage repeat local business.
- Experience-Led Retail: Hosting in-store events, such as book readings or tasting sessions, to offer a social experience that malls cannot replicate.

Conclusion
While the mall poses a serious threat, local businesses can thrive by leaning into their role as community hubs. Innovation and collaboration are essential to ensure the town centre remains more than just a ghost town.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You have seen an advertisement for an internship at a famous international news agency. Write an email to the HR manager explaining your interest, your relevant experience in journalism, and what you believe you can contribute to their digital team.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Ms. Sterling,

I am writing to formally apply for the Digital Journalism Internship at Global News Network, as advertised on your LinkedIn page. As a dedicated student of Media and Communications with a keen interest in investigative reporting, I have long admired GNN’s commitment to journalistic integrity and real-time global coverage.

During my time at university, I served as the editor of the student newspaper, where I oversaw the transition from a print-only format to a fully integrated digital platform. This experience allowed me to develop a strong command of Content Management Systems and SEO best practices. Furthermore, my recent freelance work for a local news blog involved producing short-form video content that increased their social media engagement by 40% in just three months. I am comfortable working under the pressure of tight deadlines and am adept at verifying sources in a fast-paced environment.

I believe I can contribute significantly to your digital team by providing fresh perspectives on data visualisation and social media storytelling. I am particularly eager to learn how GNN maintains its high editorial standards while competing in the 'attention economy' of modern digital media.

I have attached my portfolio and CV for your review and would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application further in an interview.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Yours sincerely,

Julian Vane

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen this post on an education blog: 'We want reviews of podcasts that are both educational and entertaining. Tell us about the podcast, the topics covered, and why it's a good way to learn on the go.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
The Past Reimagined: A Review of 'History Uncovered'

For many, history is associated with dusty textbooks and dry dates. However, the 'History Uncovered' podcast, hosted by Dr. Elena Thorne, proves that the stories of our past can be as gripping as any modern thriller. After listening to over twenty episodes during my daily commute, I can confidently say it is a masterclass in educational broadcasting.

The podcast covers a remarkably broad range of topics, from the daily lives of Roman soldiers to the secret codes used by women in the resistance during World War II. What sets it apart is the 'human-centric' approach. Instead of focusing solely on kings and battles, Dr. Thorne delves into the personal diaries and letters of ordinary people, making distant historical events feel incredibly immediate and relatable.

The production quality is exceptional. The use of subtle soundscapes and professional voice actors to read historical documents creates an immersive atmosphere that transports the listener. For a student or professional on the go, it is an ideal way to learn; the episodes are typically 30 minutes long—perfect for a bus ride or a gym session. It manages to be academically rigorous without ever feeling patronising.

If I have one criticism, it is that the host occasionally focuses too heavily on European history, and I would love to see more episodes dedicated to pre-colonial Africa or Asia. 

Overall, 'History Uncovered' is a must-listen. It turns 'dead' history into a living, breathing narrative. It is highly recommended for anyone who wants to broaden their horizons while stuck in traffic.
